patterns.name: JWT Auth Pattern
patterns.description: Complete JWT authentication with access + refresh tokens
keyFacts: Use jsonwebtoken package: jwt.sign(payload, secret, { expiresIn: '15m' })
Access token: short-lived (15min). Refresh token: long-lived (7d), stored in DB
Always verify with jwt.verify() — catch TokenExpiredError and JsonWebTokenError separately
Store refresh tokens hashed in DB, invalidate on logout
Send access token in Authorization: Bearer <token> header
Never store JWT in localStorage — use httpOnly cookies or memory
patterns.snippet: const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id, role: user.role }, process.env.JWT_SECRET, { expiresIn: '15m' });

patterns.name: Express Middleware Pattern
patterns.description: Correct async middleware and error handler setup
keyFacts: Wrap async route handlers: const asyncHandler = fn => (req, res, next) => Promise.resolve(fn(req, res, next)).catch(next)
Error middleware MUST have 4 params: (err, req, res, next)
Register error middleware LAST, after all routes
Use express.json() and express.urlencoded() before routes
cors() middleware must come BEFORE routes and auth middleware
patterns.snippet: app.use((err, req, res, next) => { const status = err.status || 500; res.status(status).json({ error: err.message }); });

patterns.name: Async/Await Error Pattern
patterns.description: Correct async error handling in Node.js
keyFacts: Every async function that can fail MUST have try/catch
Never swallow errors with empty catch blocks — at minimum log them
Use Promise.all() for parallel independent async calls — not sequential await
Promise.allSettled() when you want all results even if some fail
Unhandled promise rejections crash Node.js — always handle
process.on('unhandledRejection') is a safety net, not a replacement for try/catch

patterns.name: CORS Configuration Pattern
patterns.description: Correct CORS setup for Express APIs
keyFacts: Install: npm install cors
Add cors() BEFORE all routes: app.use(cors(options))
For credentials (cookies): set credentials: true AND specific origin (not *)
Preflight OPTIONS requests are handled automatically by cors()
In production, set origin to your exact frontend URL
patterns.snippet: app.use(cors({ origin: process.env.FRONTEND_URL, credentials: true, methods: ['GET','POST','PUT','DELETE'] }));

patterns.name: Environment Variables Pattern
patterns.description: Correct env var loading and validation
keyFacts: require('dotenv').config() must be the FIRST line in entry point
Never commit .env — add to .gitignore immediately
Validate required env vars on startup — fail fast if missing
Use defaults only for non-critical values: process.env.PORT || 3000
Never hardcode secrets, API keys, or DB credentials
patterns.snippet: const required = ['JWT_SECRET','DB_URL','PORT']; required.forEach(k => { if (!process.env[k]) throw new Error(`Missing env var: ${k}`); });

patterns.name: MongoDB/Mongoose Pattern
patterns.description: Correct Mongoose connection and schema patterns
keyFacts: Connect once at startup: mongoose.connect(process.env.MONGO_URI)
Always define schema with types and validation — don't use mixed types
Use .lean() for read-only queries — returns plain objects, faster
Handle connection errors: mongoose.connection.on('error', console.error)
Never store passwords in plain text — always hash with bcrypt before save
Use schema.pre('save') middleware for password hashing
patterns.snippet: userSchema.pre('save', async function(next) { if (this.isModified('password')) this.password = await bcrypt.hash(this.password, 12); next(); });

patterns.name: Event Emitter / Memory Leak Pattern
keyFacts: Always remove listeners when done: emitter.off('event', handler)
Use .once() for one-time listeners instead of .on() + manual removal
Default max listeners is 10 — increase with emitter.setMaxListeners(n)
In React: remove listeners in useEffect cleanup function
In Express: don't attach listeners inside request handlers — they accumulate
patterns.snippet: const handler = (data) => process(data); emitter.on('data', handler); return () => emitter.off('data', handler);

patterns.name: Input Validation Pattern
patterns.description: Validate and sanitize all user input
keyFacts: Never trust user input — validate shape, type, length, format
Use joi or zod for schema validation on request body
SQL injection: always use parameterized queries, never string concatenation
XSS: sanitize HTML output, use helmet() for security headers
Rate limiting: use express-rate-limit on auth endpoints
patterns.snippet: const schema = z.object({ email: z.string().email(), password: z.string().min(8).max(100) }); const data = schema.parse(req.body);

patterns.name: File Upload Pattern
keyFacts: Use multer for multipart form data
Always validate file type by MIME type AND file extension
Set file size limits: limits: { fileSize: 5 * 1024 * 1024 }
Never use original filename — generate a UUID filename
Store files outside web root or use cloud storage (S3)
patterns.snippet: const upload = multer({ limits: { fileSize: 5_000_000 }, fileFilter: (req, file, cb) => cb(null, ['image/jpeg','image/png'].includes(file.mimetype)) });

patterns.name: WebSocket Pattern
patterns.description: Correct socket.io setup and room management
keyFacts: Create socket server from HTTP server: const io = new Server(httpServer, { cors })
Always handle disconnect event to clean up rooms and users
Use rooms for grouping: socket.join(roomId), io.to(roomId).emit()
Authenticate socket connections in io.use() middleware
Don't store state in socket object across reconnects — use external store
